engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Modus Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Modus in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Modus K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF clogging - frequent short journeys cause blockages requiring expensive cleaning or replacement.
EGR valve failure - carbon buildup restricts airflow, triggering limp mode and warning lights.
Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade after 80k miles, causing rough running and misfires.

cards.altText: Renault Modus D4F engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - belt snaps after 72k miles if not replaced, causing catastrophic valve damage.
Coil pack failure - ignition coils fail causing misfires, rough idle and poor acceleration.
Water pump leak - pump seals degrade leading to coolant loss and overheating risk.

cards.altText: Renault Modus K4M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - tensioner failure causes belt slip and potential valve damage.
Throttle body carbon buildup - causes hesitation and stalling on city-driven examples.
Oil consumption increase - valve stem seals wear after 70k miles leading to blue exhaust smoke.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Modus Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Modus across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 65hp diesel for early Modus; reliable but prone to DPF issues on short journeys.
commonFailures: DPF clogging on urban-use vehicles requiring cleaning or replacement
EGR valve carbon buildup causing limp mode and warning lights
Injector wear after 80k miles leading to rough idle and misfires
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 700 Renault Modus engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Upgraded 86hp variant became the volume seller; balanced performance and economy for fleet use.
commonFailures: Turbocharger wear on high-mileage units causing loss of power
Swirl flap actuator failure on pre-2008 models affecting inlet manifold
Fuel pump pressure sensor faults triggering injection warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 704 Renault Modus engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Post-2007 facelift engine with Euro 5 compliance; improved emissions but added DPF complexity.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique, Dynamique S, Privilege, Initiale, Grand Modus
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure on short-trip driving causing blockage warnings
EGR cooler leaks leading to coolant loss and overheating risk
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 722 Renault Modus engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Top-spec 106hp diesel for performance-oriented Modus; sought after but requires careful maintenance.
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate sticking causing boost pressure faults and limp mode
Injector seal leaks leading to fuel dilution of engine oil
Timing chain tensioner wear on late-model units requiring preventative replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Base 75hp petrol engine; simple and affordable but timing belt critical for longevity.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 72k miles causing valve damage
Ignition coil pack failure causing misfires and rough running
Water pump seal leaks leading to coolant loss and overheating

entries.history: Revised 75hp variant with improved emissions; same reliability profile as earlier D4F.
commonFailures: Coil pack degradation causing intermittent misfires under load
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting idle quality and response
Oil consumption increase from valve stem seal wear after 70k miles

entries.title: 1.2L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: TCe turbocharged variant delivering 100hp; performance boost but added complexity.
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil feed line clogging causing turbo failure and smoke
Intercooler pipe cracks leading to boost loss and poor performance
MAP sensor faults triggering limp mode and reduced power
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4Ft 784 Renault Modus engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range 98hp naturally aspirated petrol; reliable workhorse with simple maintenance.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear causing belt slip and potential valve damage
Ignition coil failure on cylinder 3 causing persistent misfire codes
Camshaft position sensor faults triggering hard starting and stalling

entries.history: Top petrol engine with 110hp; smooth and refined but requires timely belt service.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique, Dynamique Auto, Privilege, Initiale, Initiale Auto
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure causing belt jump and valve interference
Oil pump wear leading to low oil pressure warnings at high mileage
Throttle body motor failure causing erratic idle and stalling

commonProblems.h2: Renault Modus Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Modus relies on proven K9K diesel and D4F/K4M petrol engines, but high-mileage urban use exposes known failure points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Modus.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Renault Modus Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus 1.2 Authentique, Expression, Dynamique (2004-2012, D4F 740/764); 1.4/1.6 Dynamique, Privilege (2004-2012, K4J 770/K4M 790)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 72,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: Timing belt rubber degrades with age and heat; missed service intervals cause belt teeth to shear, allowing valves to collide with pistons.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Replacement - Replace belt, tensioner, water pump before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing belt kit with tensioner and water pump replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; recommended at 72k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Repair - Repair bent valves after belt snap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve replacement, head reconditioning
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block undamaged; often not cost-effective on older Modus

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Modus; eliminates future belt risk

problems.recommendation: If your Modus is under 80k miles and the belt is overdue, preventative replacement at a specialist offers the best value. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than valve repair on a 15+ year old vehicle.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4F/K4M engine prices for your Renault Modus

problems.h4: DPF & EGR Blockage - Why Renault Modus Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus 1.5 dCi Expression, Dynamique, Privilege (2004-2012, K9K 704/722/726)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles, especially with short urban journeys
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter clogs when regeneration cycles are interrupted by short trips; EGR valve carbon buildup restricts exhaust flow, triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF Clean & EGR Service - Chemical clean and valve replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle DPF chemical clean, EGR valve replacement, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance if filter not physically damaged; short-term fix

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Replacement - New or refurbished DPF unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: DPF removal, replacement with refurbished unit, sensor calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; check injector condition first

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt diesel
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors, DPF-ready, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Modus; addresses injector wear and DPF risk together

problems.recommendation: For Modus used mainly for short trips, a DPF delete (where legal) or frequent motorway runs can extend life. If the engine has over 100k miles, a rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors offers better long-term value than repeated DPF repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Modus diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Ignition Coil Failure - Why Renault Modus Petrol Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus 1.2/1.4/1.6 petrol variants (2004-2012, D4F/K4J/K4M codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles, often sooner on stop-start urban use
problems.rootCause: Coil pack insulation breaks down from heat cycling; moisture ingress accelerates failure, causing intermittent misfires and rough idle.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Coil Pack Replacement - Replace faulty coil(s) and spark plugs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, coil replacement, spark plug renewal, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for isolated coil failure; replace all coils if over 80k miles

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Wiring Harness Repair - Fix damaged coil wiring
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Wiring loom inspection, connector repair or harness replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses recurring coil failures caused by chafed or corroded wiring

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new coils, harness check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires caused piston/cylinder damage or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: Replace all four coils as a set if one fails on a Modus over 70k miles - the others are likely to follow soon. If misfires have caused catalytic converter damage, a rebuilt engine may be more economical than multiple component repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4F/K4J engine prices for your Renault Modus

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why Renault Modus 1.5 dCi Loses Boost
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus 1.5 dCi Dynamique, Privilege, Initiale (2004-2012, K9K 722/726)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-130,000 miles, accelerated by poor oil change intervals
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearing wear from oil starvation or contamination; wastegate sticking causes over/under-boost faults and limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Actuator Repair - Replace wastegate actuator only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator replacement, boost pressure calibration, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes limp mode if turbo core is healthy; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Rebuild - Recondition turbocharger unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, specialist rebuild with new bearings/seals, refit and test
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if engine block and injectors are in good condition

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt diesel
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt K9K with upgraded turbo, injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Modus; addresses turbo, injector and bearing wear together

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is caught early (smoke but no bearing noise), a specialist rebuild offers good value. If the engine has over 110k miles or shows injector wear symptoms, a rebuilt K9K with upgraded turbo provides better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K turbo diesel Renault Modus engine replacement

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Why Renault Modus 1.5 dCi Runs Rough
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus 1.5 dCi all variants (2004-2012, K9K series)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector seals degrade allowing fuel dilution of oil; nozzle wear causes poor atomisation, rough idle and increased emissions.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Injector Seal Replacement - Replace leaking injector seals only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, seal replacement, coding adaptation, oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes oil dilution if injectors themselves are still functional

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Replacement - Replace faulty injectors individually
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic coding, injector replacement, ECU adaptation, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only 1-2 injectors faulty and engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt K9K Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt diesel
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple injectors faulty or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: If oil analysis shows fuel dilution, replace all injector seals as preventative maintenance. If injectors are faulty and the engine has over 90k miles, a rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors offers better value than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K diesel engine prices for your Renault Modus

problems.h4: Engine Knocking Noise - Bottom End Failure on High-Mileage Modus
problems.affectedModels: Renault Modus all engine types with over 100k miles (2004-2012)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 100,000-150,000 miles, accelerated by missed oil changes
problems.rootCause: Connecting rod bearing wear from oil starvation or contamination; bearing failure causes knock, metal debris circulates damaging crankshaft and cylinders.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Not Applicable - Knock indicates catastrophic damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: No minor repair viable once knocking is audible
repairOptions.longevity: Diagnostic essential to confirm extent of damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Short Block Repair - Replace bearings, crank, pistons
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, short block rebuild with new bearings, machining, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only cost-effective on low-mileage Modus with otherwise perfect condition

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for most Modus; eliminates bottom end risk and includes warranty

problems.recommendation: Once knocking is audible, engine replacement is almost always more economical than repair on a 15+ year old Modus. A rebuilt engine with upgraded bearings provides peace of mind and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Renault Modus engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Modus
finalCta.paragraph: The Renault Modus is a practical city MPV, but its engines have predictable failure patterns at high mileage. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Modus specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Modus Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Modus and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Modus Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Modus in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Renault Modus 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel was the backbone of the Modus range, offered from launch in 2004 through to end of production in 2012 across multiple power outputs to suit different trim levels and usage patterns. [[17]]
commonFailurePoints: turbo wastegate sticking on high-output variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Modus 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Modus 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.2L D4F petrol powered base Authentique and Expression trims, offering affordable running costs but requiring strict timing belt adherence to avoid catastrophic failure. [[17]]
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 72k miles
items.cta: Compare Renault Modus 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Modus 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L K4J offered a mid-range power option for Dynamique and Privilege trims, balancing performance and simplicity with naturally aspirated reliability. [[1]]
items.cta: Compare Renault Modus 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Modus 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 1.6L K4M petrol delivered smooth 110-113hp performance for top-spec Dynamique and Initiale trims, with automatic transmission available on later models. [[2]]
commonFailurePoints: oil pump wear at high mileage
throttle body motor faults causing stalling
items.cta: Compare Renault Modus 1.6L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Renault Modus needs? All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Renault Modus specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Renault Modus Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Modus engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.2 petrol Authentique to the latest 1.5 dCi Initiale.

items.title: Renault Modus Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered most Modus models from 2004-2012, offering strong economy for high-mileage drivers but requiring careful DPF/EGR maintenance on urban-use vehicles. [[17]]
items.descriptor: The 1.5L K9K diesel powered most Modus models from 2004-2012, offering strong economy for high-mileage drivers but requiring careful DPF/EGR maintenance on urban-use vehicles. [[17]]
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Modus Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K 700/704 - 65-86hp base/mid variants, high UK replacement demand on Expression/Dynamique trims
K9K 722/726 - 88-106hp facelift variants, DPF-equipped with known regeneration issues on short journeys
K9K 740 - Grand Modus extended-wheelbase application, shared core architecture with standard Modus
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (60+ mpg claimed) on long journeys
DPF clogging and EGR faults on predominantly urban-use vehicles
High UK replacement demand on 1.5 dCi Expression/Dynamique variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Modus Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Modus Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Modus variants used simple 1.2L D4F, 1.4L K4J and 1.6L K4M engines offering affordable running costs but requiring strict timing belt service adherence to avoid catastrophic failure. [[17]]
items.descriptor: Petrol Modus variants used simple 1.2L D4F, 1.4L K4J and 1.6L K4M engines offering affordable running costs but requiring strict timing belt service adherence to avoid catastrophic failure. [[17]]
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Modus Petrol Engine Families
families: D4F 740/764 - 75hp base petrol, timing belt critical, coil pack failures common on high-mileage units
K4J 770 - 98hp mid-range petrol, reliable if belt serviced, coil pack issues on cylinder 3
K4M 790/791 - 110-113hp flagship petrol, smooth performance but tensioner wear requires attention
foundIn: Phase 1 Modus (2004-2007) - D4F 740, K4J 770, K4M 790, all naturally aspirated
Phase 2 Modus (2007-2012) - D4F 764/786, K4M 791/800, plus D4Ft TCe turbo variant
knownFor: Affordable purchase price and simple maintenance on naturally aspirated units
Timing belt failure risk if not replaced at 72k miles/5 years across all petrol codes
Ignition coil pack degradation causing misfires on stop-start urban driving
items.cta: Compare Renault Modus Petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Modus Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Renault Modus was not offered with hybrid or electrified powertrains in the UK market during its 2004-2012 production run. [[48]]
items.descriptor: The Renault Modus was not offered with hybrid or electrified powertrains in the UK market during its 2004-2012 production run. [[48]]
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Modus Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No mild hybrid, plug-in hybrid or full electric Modus variants were sold in the UK
All UK Modus engines are conventional petrol or diesel internal combustion units
Replacement options focus on rebuilt, reconditioned or used conventional engines only
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Modus conventional engine availability

engineYears.h2: Renault Modus Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Modus production timeline (2004-2012), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Modus launched with simple naturally aspirated petrol engines and early K9K diesels without DPF, establishing the model's practical city-MPV positioning.
keyChanges: Launch of Renault Modus in UK market with Phase 1 body and chassis
Introduction of K9K diesel without DPF for fleet and high-mileage buyers
Base D4F 1.2L and K4J 1.4L petrol engines for budget-conscious private owners
knownFor: Simple, affordable engines with low purchase price appeal
Timing belt critical on all petrol codes - failure causes valve damage
Early K9K diesels robust but injector wear emerges after 80k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2007 Renault Modus replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Modus 1.2 timing belt replacement • Renault Modus 1.5 dCi injector repair • Renault Modus coil pack misfire fix
enquiries: Renault Modus coil pack misfire fix

years.description: The 2007 facelift introduced Euro 5 emissions compliance, DPF on diesels and the TCe turbo petrol, raising complexity but improving refinement and economy.
keyChanges: Introduction of DPF on all 1.5 dCi diesel variants for Euro 5 compliance
Launch of D4Ft TCe 1.2L turbo petrol delivering 100hp for Dynamique trim
Revised K9K variants (722/726) with updated injectors and EGR systems
Grand Modus extended-wheelbase variant launched with shared engine range
mainEngines: K9K 722/726/740 - 1.5L diesel 88-106hp with DPF (all diesel trims + Grand Modus)
knownFor: DPF regeneration issues on urban-use diesel Modus causing limp mode
TCe turbo reliability concerns with oil feed line clogging on early units
Improved refinement and emissions but increased repair complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2012 Renault Modus replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Modus DPF blockage repair • Renault Modus TCe turbo failure • Renault Modus 1.6 Initiale engine replacement

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Renault Modus was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Renault Modus specialists.

faq.h2: Renault Modus Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Modus engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Renault Modus engine prices range from £500 for a 1.2L petrol to £1,400 for a 1.5L diesel, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£850 labour depending on location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: 1.2L D4F petrol rebuilt: £500-£900 supply only
1.5L K9K diesel rebuilt: £650-£1,400 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£850 to engine price
Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Renault Modus engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit my Renault Modus?
items.answer: UK Renault Modus engines use K9K (1.5L diesel), D4F (1.2L petrol), K4J (1.4L petrol) or K4M (1.6L petrol) codes. Enter your registration above to verify the exact code for your vehicle.
keyPoints: Engine code is stamped on block and in V5C registration document
Using wrong code risks incompatibility with ECU and emissions systems
items.cta: Find your Renault Modus engine code and compare quotes

items.question: Do you supply and fit Renault Modus engines?
items.answer: Yes, our UK network offers both supply-only and full supply & fit Renault Modus engine replacement. Supply & fit includes professional installation, fluids, coding and road test at a local specialist.
keyPoints: Supply-only: engine delivered to your chosen garage
Supply & fit: engine plus professional installation nationwide
All options include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Book online or request quotes to compare local specialist pricing
items.cta: Find Renault Modus engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault Modus recon engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Renault Modus engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the installing specialist.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Keep service records and use correct oil to maintain coverage
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare Renault Modus recon engine warranty options

items.question: How long does Renault Modus engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days. Full supply & fit replacement usually completes in 1-2 days at a specialist garage, depending on parts availability and booking schedule.
keyPoints: Engine dispatch: 1-3 working days after order confirmation
Supply & fit installation: 1-2 days at specialist garage
Complex variants (auto, TCe turbo) may require additional coding time
Confirm exact timeline with your chosen supplier when booking
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Modus engine replacement with turnaround times

items.question: Can I replace my Modus engine with a different code?
items.answer: Engine code swaps on Renault Modus are not recommended without specialist ECU and emissions system reprogramming. Stick to the original code for your variant to ensure compatibility and MOT compliance.
keyPoints: ECU coding and immobiliser must match original engine code
Emissions systems (DPF, EGR) are code-specific and MOT-critical
Swapping codes risks failed MOT, warning lights and poor performance
Use our registration lookup to confirm the correct code for your Modus
items.cta: Verify your Renault Modus engine code before ordering replacement

items.question: Are used Renault Modus engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Renault Modus engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, accident-damaged vehicles and compression tested before sale. Always request mileage verification and warranty terms from the supplier.
keyPoints: Request compression test results and service history with used engine
Verify mileage via diagnostic scan or documented service records
Typical used engine warranty: 3-6 months parts and labour
Rebuilt engines offer longer warranty and upgraded components for similar cost
items.cta: Check tested used Renault Modus engine availability with warranty

items.question: What is the difference between recon and remanufactured Modus engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Renault Modus engines replace worn parts to restore function; remanufactured units rebuild to original factory spec with new components throughout, offering like-new reliability at higher cost.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: clean, inspect, replace worn parts, test
Remanufactured: full machining, new bearings/pistons, factory-spec rebuild
Remanufactured typically costs 20-40% more but offers longer potential lifespan
Both include minimum 12-month warranty when supplied through EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare recon vs remanufactured Renault Modus engine options

items.question: Do Renault Modus Quickshift engines cost more to replace?
items.answer: Renault Modus Quickshift (automated manual) variants may incur slightly higher replacement costs due to transmission compatibility checks and ECU adaptation requirements during installation.
keyPoints: Quickshift models require transmission compatibility verification
ECU adaptation and clutch calibration add to labour time
Typical price premium: £100-£250 over manual transmission equivalents
Confirm Quickshift compatibility with supplier before ordering engine
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Modus Quickshift engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Renault Modus engine code?
items.answer: Your Renault Modus engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near timing cover), listed in the V5C registration document under "engine number", and can be verified instantly by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Check V5C registration document under "engine number" field
Engine code stamped on block near timing cover or oil filter housing
Diagnostic scan tool can read engine code from ECU if accessible
Use our free registration lookup for instant code verification
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Renault Modus engine code instantly
